methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $140M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $700M - $3.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Panthalassa is a Series B climate and energy company founded by Garth Sheldon-Coulson and Brian Moffat, having raised $140M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws from a peer set spanning one public comparable (LNZA) and eight private-stage companies, including KoBold Metals, Electric Hydrogen, and Amogy—all operating in the same sector at Series C stage.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x multiplier to the $140M total capital raised, yielding an implied valuation of $266M based on typical Series B advancement dynamics. Peer-median analysis references three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables: KoBold Metals at $3.0B, Electric Hydrogen at $1.0B, and Amogy at $700M. The median of this peer cohort is $1.0B, with a range spanning $700M to $3.0B.

The combined triangulation supports a valuation range of $226M (low) to $1.1B (high), with a median of $633M. The wide dispersion between stage-progression ($266M) and peer-median ($1.0B) reflects material uncertainty around comparable selection and stage-appropriate discounting within the climate-tech sector. Key uncertainties include the applicability of Series C peer valuations to a Series B asset and the heterogeneity of business models and capital intensity across the peer set. Additional diligence on unit economics and technology risk would narrow the range materially.
